item.title: Thoughts after GRE test
item.description:
Last weekend, I went to Jiangxi University of Financial and Economics, Nanchang, Jiangxi Province to take my GRE test. There was limited time for my GRE preparation during this semester, so the score was not good: only 142 for verbal and 169 for quantitative (AW is under review). But when I think about lots of universities have waived GRE score for graduate application, I’m kind of relieved–at least I own a GRE score.

It is almost two years since I decided to apply a PhD. It is a impressive journey to prepare for so many materials under a heavy work, but now I finally make them! I also learn two my-own-philosophy from this process:

Now matter how difficult things are, it can absolutely be done by making small plans.

Hold horses and stay calm. (年轻人要沉住气)

Also, this is the first time for me to travel to Nanchang, it is a beautiful place and Nanchang Banfen (南昌拌粉) is really delicious. Hope the next time I can stay longer there.

item.title: Say something about me
item.description: Outside the research, I’m an outgoing person who is easy to get along well with. I have various interests such as jogging, playing basketball, and computer games (such as LOL and Steam games). I am also a music-lover who likes composing songs ♫ in my spare time and can play several instruments like Erhu, Guitar, and Piano. I am also a dog fancier, especially Shiba Inu.
